  • Python Implementation Notes

Difference Array

Overview

Difference Array is a technique for efficiently performing range update operations on an array. Instead of updating each element individually (O(n) per update), we can perform range updates in O(1) time and reconstruct the final array in O(n) time.

Key Properties

  • Time Complexity:
    • Range Update: O(1)
    • Build Difference Array: O(n)
    • Reconstruct Original: O(n)
    • Multiple Updates: O(m) for m updates
  • Space Complexity: O(n) for storing difference array
  • Core Idea: Store differences between consecutive elements to enable efficient range updates
  • When to Use: Multiple range update operations, interval modifications, booking systems, resource allocation

References

  • Difference Array Visualization
  • fucking algorithm - Difference Array
  • Prefix Sum vs Difference Array

Problem Categories

Pattern 1: Basic Range Updates

  • Description: Add/subtract a value to all elements in a range
  • Recognition: “Update range [i, j]”, “increment by val”, “modify interval”
  • Examples: LC 370, LC 1109, LC 1893
  • Template: Use Basic Difference Array Template

Pattern 2: Resource Allocation

  • Description: Track resource usage across intervals
  • Recognition: “Booking”, “capacity”, “overlapping intervals”
  • Examples: LC 1094, LC 731, LC 732
  • Template: Use Resource Tracking Template

Pattern 3: Event Timeline

  • Description: Process events happening at different times
  • Recognition: “Start/end times”, “scheduling”, “timeline”
  • Examples: LC 253, LC 1851, LC 2021
  • Template: Use Event Processing Template

Pattern 4: 2D Difference Array

  • Description: Range updates on 2D matrices
  • Recognition: “Rectangle updates”, “2D range modifications”
  • Examples: LC 2132, LC 2536
  • Template: Use 2D Difference Array Template

Templates & Algorithms

Template Comparison Table

Template Type Use Case Update Time Query Time Space When to Use
Basic Difference Range updates O(1) O(n) rebuild O(n) Multiple range updates
With Prefix Sum Range updates + queries O(1) O(1) O(n) Updates and queries
2D Difference Matrix range updates O(1) O(mn) rebuild O(mn) 2D range updates
Lazy Propagation Dynamic queries O(log n) O(log n) O(n) Many queries between updates

Universal Difference Array Template

1def difference_array_template(nums, updates):
2    """
3    Universal template for difference array problems
4    nums: original array
5    updates: list of [start, end, value] operations
6    """
7    n = len(nums)
8    # Build difference array
9    diff = [0] * n
10    diff[0] = nums[0]
11    for i in range(1, n):
12        diff[i] = nums[i] - nums[i-1]
13    
14    # Apply range updates in O(1) each
15    for start, end, val in updates:
16        diff[start] += val
17        if end + 1 < n:
18            diff[end + 1] -= val
19    
20    # Reconstruct final array
21    result = [0] * n
22    result[0] = diff[0]
23    for i in range(1, n):
24        result[i] = result[i-1] + diff[i]
25    
26    return result

Template 1: Basic Difference Array

1class DifferenceArray:
2    def __init__(self, nums):
3        """Initialize difference array from original array"""
4        self.n = len(nums)
5        self.diff = [0] * self.n
6        
7        # Build difference array
8        self.diff[0] = nums[0]
9        for i in range(1, self.n):
10            self.diff[i] = nums[i] - nums[i-1]
11    
12    def update(self, start, end, val):
13        """Add val to all elements in range [start, end] in O(1)"""
14        self.diff[start] += val
15        if end + 1 < self.n:
16            self.diff[end + 1] -= val
17    
18    def get_result(self):
19        """Reconstruct the final array in O(n)"""
20        result = [0] * self.n
21        result[0] = self.diff[0]
22        for i in range(1, self.n):
23            result[i] = result[i-1] + self.diff[i]
24        return result

Template 2: Resource Allocation

1def check_resource_allocation(intervals, capacity, resource_field=2):
2    """
3    Check if resource allocation is valid
4    intervals: [[start, end, resource_needed], ...]
5    capacity: maximum available resource
6    """
7    # Find the range of positions
8    max_pos = max(interval[1] for interval in intervals) + 1
9    diff = [0] * max_pos
10    
11    # Apply all resource allocations
12    for interval in intervals:
13        start, end, resource = interval[0], interval[1], interval[resource_field]
14        diff[start] += resource
15        if end + 1 < max_pos:
16            diff[end + 1] -= resource
17    
18    # Check if any position exceeds capacity
19    current = 0
20    for i in range(max_pos):
21        current += diff[i]
22        if current > capacity:
23            return False
24    
25    return True

Template 3: Event Timeline

1def process_events(events):
2    """
3    Process events on a timeline
4    events: [[start_time, end_time, value], ...]
5    Returns: timeline with accumulated values
6    """
7    if not events:
8        return []
9    
10    # Create timeline
11    max_time = max(e[1] for e in events) + 1
12    timeline = [0] * max_time
13    
14    # Process each event
15    for start, end, value in events:
16        timeline[start] += value
17        if end + 1 < max_time:
18            timeline[end + 1] -= value
19    
20    # Calculate prefix sum to get actual values
21    for i in range(1, max_time):
22        timeline[i] += timeline[i-1]
23    
24    return timeline

Template 4: 2D Difference Array

1class DifferenceArray2D:
2    def __init__(self, matrix):
3        """Initialize 2D difference array"""
4        self.m, self.n = len(matrix), len(matrix[0])
5        self.diff = [[0] * self.n for _ in range(self.m)]
6        
7        # Build 2D difference array
8        for i in range(self.m):
9            for j in range(self.n):
10                self.diff[i][j] = matrix[i][j]
11                if i > 0:
12                    self.diff[i][j] -= matrix[i-1][j]
13                if j > 0:
14                    self.diff[i][j] -= matrix[i][j-1]
15                if i > 0 and j > 0:
16                    self.diff[i][j] += matrix[i-1][j-1]
17    
18    def update(self, r1, c1, r2, c2, val):
19        """Add val to all elements in rectangle [r1,c1] to [r2,c2]"""
20        self.diff[r1][c1] += val
21        if r2 + 1 < self.m:
22            self.diff[r2 + 1][c1] -= val
23        if c2 + 1 < self.n:
24            self.diff[r1][c2 + 1] -= val
25        if r2 + 1 < self.m and c2 + 1 < self.n:
26            self.diff[r2 + 1][c2 + 1] += val
27    
28    def get_result(self):
29        """Reconstruct the final 2D array"""
30        result = [[0] * self.n for _ in range(self.m)]
31        
32        for i in range(self.m):
33            for j in range(self.n):
34                result[i][j] = self.diff[i][j]
35                if i > 0:
36                    result[i][j] += result[i-1][j]
37                if j > 0:
38                    result[i][j] += result[i][j-1]
39                if i > 0 and j > 0:
40                    result[i][j] -= result[i-1][j-1]
41        
42        return result

Template 5: Optimized with Coordinates Compression

1def difference_array_compressed(updates):
2    """
3    Handle large coordinate space with compression
4    updates: [[start, end, value], ...]
5    """
6    # Collect all unique points
7    points = set()
8    for start, end, _ in updates:
9        points.add(start)
10        points.add(end + 1)
11    
12    # Sort and create mapping
13    sorted_points = sorted(points)
14    point_to_idx = {p: i for i, p in enumerate(sorted_points)}
15    
16    # Apply updates on compressed coordinates
17    n = len(sorted_points)
18    diff = [0] * n
19    
20    for start, end, val in updates:
21        start_idx = point_to_idx[start]
22        end_idx = point_to_idx.get(end + 1, n)
23        diff[start_idx] += val
24        if end_idx < n:
25            diff[end_idx] -= val
26    
27    # Calculate values at each point
28    for i in range(1, n):
29        diff[i] += diff[i-1]
30    
31    # Return results with original coordinates
32    result = {}
33    for i, point in enumerate(sorted_points[:-1]):  # Exclude the last dummy point
34        if diff[i] != 0:
35            result[point] = diff[i]
36    
37    return result

1) General form

1
2// java
3// https://labuladong.online/algo/data-structure/diff-array/
4
5// 差分数组工具类
6// V1
7class Difference {
8    // 差分数组
9    private int[] diff;
10    
11    // 输入一个初始数组，区间操作将在这个数组上进行
12    public Difference(int[] nums) {
13        assert nums.length > 0;
14        diff = new int[nums.length];
15        // 根据初始数组构造差分数组
16        diff[0] = nums[0];
17        for (int i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) {
18            diff[i] = nums[i] - nums[i - 1];
19        }
20    }
21
22    // 给闭区间 [i, j] 增加 val（可以是负数）
23    public void increment(int i, int j, int val) {
24        diff[i] += val;
25        if (j + 1 < diff.length) {
26            diff[j + 1] -= val;
27        }
28    }
29
30    // 返回结果数组
31    public int[] result() {
32        int[] res = new int[diff.length];
33        // 根据差分数组构造结果数组
34        res[0] = diff[0];
35        for (int i = 1; i < diff.length; i++) {
36            res[i] = res[i - 1] + diff[i];
37        }
38        return res;
39    }
40}

1// V2
2// https://github.com/yennanliu/CS_basics/blob/master/leetcode_java/src/main/java/AlgorithmJava/DifferenceArray.java
3
4
5// method
6public int[] getDifferenceArray(int[][] input, int n) {
7
8/** LC 1109. Corporate Flight Bookings input : [start, end, seats]
9 *
10 *  NOTE !!!
11 *
12 *   in java, index start from 0;
13 *   but in LC 1109, index start from 1
14 *
15 */
16int[] tmp = new int[n + 1];
17for (int[] x : input) {
18  int start = x[0];
19  int end = x[1];
20  int seats = x[2];
21
22  // add
23  tmp[start] += seats;
24
25  // subtract
26  if (end + 1 <= n) {
27    tmp[end + 1] -= seats;
28  }
29}
30
31for (int i = 1; i < tmp.length; i++) {
32  //tmp[i] = tmp[i - 1] + tmp[i];
33    tmp[i] += tmp[i - 1];
34}
35
36return Arrays.copyOfRange(tmp, 1, n+1);
37}

Problems by Pattern

Pattern-Based Problem Classification

Pattern 1: Basic Range Update Problems

Problem LC # Difficulty Key Technique Template
Range Addition 370 Medium Basic difference array Template 1
Corporate Flight Bookings 1109 Medium Range updates Template 1
Range Addition II 598 Easy Minimum overlap Template 1
Apply Operations to Make All Array Elements Equal to Zero 2772 Medium Range updates Template 1

Pattern 2: Resource Allocation Problems

Problem LC # Difficulty Key Technique Template
Car Pooling 1094 Medium Capacity check Template 2
Meeting Rooms II 253 Medium Timeline events Template 2
My Calendar I 729 Medium Interval booking Template 2
My Calendar II 731 Medium Double booking Template 2
My Calendar III 732 Hard K-booking Template 2

Pattern 3: Event Timeline Problems

Problem LC # Difficulty Key Technique Template
Number of Flowers in Full Bloom 2251 Hard Timeline query Template 3
Describe the Painting 2158 Medium Color mixing Template 3
Maximum Population Year 1854 Easy Timeline count Template 3
Count Positions on Street With Required Brightness 2021 Medium Light coverage Template 3

Pattern 4: 2D Difference Array Problems

Problem LC # Difficulty Key Technique Template
Stamping the Grid 2132 Hard 2D range update Template 4
Increment Submatrices by One 2536 Medium Rectangle updates Template 4

Complete Problem List by Difficulty

Easy Problems (Foundation)

  • LC 598: Range Addition II - Find minimum affected area
  • LC 1854: Maximum Population Year - Simple timeline
  • LC 1893: Check if All Integers in Range Are Covered - Range coverage

Medium Problems (Core)

  • LC 370: Range Addition - Classic difference array
  • LC 1109: Corporate Flight Bookings - Flight seat allocation
  • LC 1094: Car Pooling - Resource capacity validation
  • LC 253: Meeting Rooms II - Minimum rooms needed
  • LC 729: My Calendar I - No double booking
  • LC 731: My Calendar II - Allow one double booking
  • LC 2021: Street Light Brightness - Range illumination
  • LC 2158: Amount of New Area Painted - Color segments
  • LC 2536: Increment Submatrices by One - 2D updates
  • LC 2772: Apply Operations to Array - Make all zeros

Hard Problems (Advanced)

  • LC 732: My Calendar III - Maximum K-booking
  • LC 2132: Stamping the Grid - 2D stamp validation
  • LC 2251: Number of Flowers in Full Bloom - Point queries on timeline

1-1) Basic OP

2) LC Example

2-1) Range Addition

1// java
2// LC 370
3
4
5// V0
6// IDEA : DIFFERENCE ARRAY
7public static int[] getModifiedArray(int length, int[][] updates) {
8
9int[] tmp = new int[length + 1]; // or new int[length]; both works
10for (int[] x : updates) {
11  int start = x[0];
12  int end = x[1];
13  int amount = x[2];
14
15  // add
16  tmp[start] += amount;
17
18  // subtract (remove the "adding affect" on "NEXT" element)
19  /**
20   * NOTE !!!
21   *
22   * <p>we remove the "adding affect" on NEXT element (e.g. end + 1)
23   */
24  if (end + 1 < length) { // NOTE !!! use `end + 1`
25    tmp[end + 1] -= amount;
26  }
27}
28
29// prepare final result
30for (int i = 1; i < tmp.length; i++) {
31  tmp[i] += tmp[i - 1];
32}
33
34return Arrays.copyOfRange(tmp, 0, length); // return the sub array between 0, lengh index
35}
36
37// V1
38class Solution {
39    public int[] getModifiedArray(int length, int[][] updates) {
40        // nums 初始化为全 0
41        int[] nums = new int[length];
42        // 构造差分解法
43        Difference df = new Difference(nums);
44        
45        for (int[] update : updates) {
46            int i = update[0];
47            int j = update[1];
48            int val = update[2];
49            df.increment(i, j, val);
50        }
51        
52        return df.result();
53    }
54}

2-2) Corporate Flight Bookings

1// java
2// LC 1109
3
4// V1
5class Solution {
6    public int[] corpFlightBookings(int[][] bookings, int n) {
7        // nums 初始化为全 0
8        int[] nums = new int[n];
9        // 构造差分解法
10        Difference df = new Difference(nums);
11
12        for (int[] booking : bookings) {
13            // 注意转成数组索引要减一哦
14            int i = booking[0] - 1;
15            int j = booking[1] - 1;
16            int val = booking[2];
17            // 对区间 nums[i..j] 增加 val
18            df.increment(i, j, val);
19        }
20        // 返回最终的结果数组
21        return df.result();
22    }
23}

2-3) Car Pooling

1// java
2// LC 1094
3// https://leetcode.com/problems/car-pooling/description/
4
5class Solution {
6    public boolean carPooling(int[][] trips, int capacity) {
7        // 最多有 1001 个车站
8        int[] nums = new int[1001];
9
10        // 构造差分解法
11        Difference df = new Difference(nums);
12
13        for (int[] trip : trips) {
14            // 乘客数量
15            int val = trip[0];
16
17            // 第 trip[1] 站乘客上车
18            int i = trip[1];
19
20            // 第 trip[2] 站乘客已经下车，
21            // 即乘客在车上的区间是 [trip[1], trip[2] - 1]
22            int j = trip[2] - 1;
23
24            // 进行区间操作
25            df.increment(i, j, val);
26        }
27
28        int[] res = df.result();
29
30        // 客车自始至终都不应该超载
31        for (int i = 0; i < res.length; i++) {
32            if (capacity < res[i]) {
33                return false;
34            }
35        }
36        return true;
37    }
38}

Pattern Selection Strategy

1Difference Array Problem Analysis Flowchart:
2
31. Does the problem involve range updates?
4   ├── YES → Check update pattern
5   │   ├── Multiple ranges need same update? → Use Difference Array
6   │   ├── Single element updates? → Use direct array
7   │   └── Need immediate query results? → Consider Segment Tree
8   └── NO → Not a difference array problem
9
102. What type of range operation?
11   ├── Add/Subtract constant to range → Basic Difference Array (Template 1)
12   ├── Track resource usage → Resource Allocation (Template 2)
13   ├── Timeline/Event processing → Event Timeline (Template 3)
14   └── 2D matrix updates → 2D Difference Array (Template 4)
15
163. Space/Time Trade-offs:
17   ├── Large coordinate space? → Use Coordinate Compression (Template 5)
18   ├── Many queries between updates? → Consider Lazy Propagation
19   └── Only final result needed? → Basic Difference Array
20
214. Special Considerations:
22   ├── Online vs Offline → Difference array is offline
23   ├── Need range queries? → Combine with Prefix Sum
24   └── Overlapping intervals? → Check maximum overlap

Decision Framework

  1. Identify range updates: Look for “update range [l, r]” operations
  2. Count operations: Multiple updates = good for difference array
  3. Check query pattern: Final result only vs intermediate queries
  4. Consider alternatives: Segment tree for dynamic queries

Summary & Quick Reference

Complexity Quick Reference

Operation Time Complexity Space Complexity Notes
Build Difference Array O(n) O(n) From original array
Range Update O(1) O(1) Add value to range
Reconstruct Array O(n) O(1) Get final result
M Updates + Reconstruct O(m + n) O(n) Total complexity
2D Range Update O(1) O(1) Rectangle update
2D Reconstruct O(mn) O(1) Get final matrix

Template Quick Reference

Template Best For Avoid When Key Pattern
Basic Difference Multiple range updates Single updates diff[i] = arr[i] - arr[i-1]
Resource Allocation Capacity constraints Unbounded resources Check max usage
Event Timeline Time-based events Spatial problems Timeline array
2D Difference Matrix range updates 1D problems 4-point update
Coordinate Compression Large sparse space Dense arrays Map coordinates

Common Patterns & Tricks

Pattern: Range Update Formula

1# To add val to range [start, end]:
2diff[start] += val
3if end + 1 < n:
4    diff[end + 1] -= val

Pattern: Off-by-One for Intervals

1# If passengers get off at station x, they're on board [start, x-1]
2# If event ends at time t, it's active [start, t]
3# Be careful with inclusive/exclusive boundaries!
4
5# Car pooling example:
6for passengers, pickup, dropoff in trips:
7    diff[pickup] += passengers
8    diff[dropoff] -= passengers  # Not dropoff-1!

Pattern: Maximum Concurrent Events

1def max_concurrent(intervals):
2    events = []
3    for start, end in intervals:
4        events.append((start, 1))   # Start event
5        events.append((end + 1, -1)) # End event
6    
7    events.sort()
8    max_concurrent = current = 0
9    
10    for time, delta in events:
11        current += delta
12        max_concurrent = max(max_concurrent, current)
13    
14    return max_concurrent

Problem-Solving Steps

  1. Identify range operations: Look for [start, end] updates
  2. Initialize difference array: Usually all zeros
  3. Apply updates: O(1) per update using formula
  4. Reconstruct if needed: Prefix sum to get final array
  5. Validate constraints: Check capacity, overlaps, etc.

Common Mistakes & Tips

🚫 Common Mistakes:

  • Off-by-one errors: Careful with inclusive/exclusive ranges
  • Array bounds: Check end+1 before updating
  • Initial values: Don’t forget original array values
  • 2D formula errors: Four points need correct signs
  • Overflow: Large values × many updates

✅ Best Practices:

  • Use clear variable names: start/end not i/j
  • Comment boundary logic: Explain inclusive/exclusive
  • Test edge cases: Empty ranges, full array updates
  • Consider compression: For large coordinate spaces
  • Validate early: Check impossible cases first

Interview Tips

  1. Recognize the pattern: “Update multiple ranges” → Difference array
  2. Explain the technique: “Convert range updates to point updates”
  3. Mention trade-offs: Offline processing, O(n) reconstruction
  4. Know alternatives: Segment tree for online queries
  5. Handle edge cases: Empty input, single element, overlapping ranges

Related Topics

  • Prefix Sum: Opposite operation, range queries
  • Segment Tree: When need both updates and queries
  • Fenwick Tree (BIT): Alternative for range operations
  • Sweep Line: Related technique for interval problems
  • Coordinate Compression: Handling large sparse ranges

Java Implementation Notes

1// Java Difference Array Class
2class Difference {
3    private int[] diff;
4    
5    public Difference(int[] nums) {
6        diff = new int[nums.length];
7        diff[0] = nums[0];
8        for (int i = 1; i < nums.length; i++) {
9            diff[i] = nums[i] - nums[i-1];
10        }
11    }
12    
13    public void increment(int i, int j, int val) {
14        diff[i] += val;
15        if (j + 1 < diff.length) {
16            diff[j + 1] -= val;
17        }
18    }
19    
20    public int[] result() {
21        int[] res = new int[diff.length];
22        res[0] = diff[0];
23        for (int i = 1; i < diff.length; i++) {
24            res[i] = res[i-1] + diff[i];
25        }
26        return res;
27    }
28}

Python Implementation Notes

1# Python class implementation
2class DifferenceArray:
3    def __init__(self, nums):
4        self.n = len(nums)
5        self.diff = [0] * self.n
6        if nums:
7            self.diff[0] = nums[0]
8            for i in range(1, self.n):
9                self.diff[i] = nums[i] - nums[i-1]
10    
11    def update(self, start, end, val):
12        self.diff[start] += val
13        if end + 1 < self.n:
14            self.diff[end + 1] -= val
15    
16    def get_result(self):
17        result = [self.diff[0]]
18        for i in range(1, self.n):
19            result.append(result[-1] + self.diff[i])
20        return result

Must-Know Problems for Interviews: LC 370, 1109, 1094, 253, 732 Advanced Problems: LC 732, 2132, 2251 Keywords: difference array, range update, interval modification, sweep line, prefix sum
